page: Google Cloud Platform x4-1440-24t-metal is a bare-metal server instance belonging to the x4 server family. It features an x86_64 CPU architecture with 1440 dedicated virtual CPUs and a total memory capacity of 24576 GB. This configuration yields a high memory-to-core ratio of 17.07 GB of RAM per vCPU.

This server configuration does not include local NVMe or SSD storage, meaning all storage requirements must be met via network-attached storage options. Additionally, the instance does not feature any integrated GPUs, and it comes with zero complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

Given the massive memory footprint and high count of dedicated physical resources, this bare-metal instance represents a highly specialized hardware class. It is designed for extreme resource density, making it qualitatively cost-efficient for consolidating massive enterprise workloads that would otherwise require multi-node clusters. The server is suited for large-scale in-memory databases, massive enterprise resource planning systems, and heavy data-processing pipelines that require substantial memory and compute resources simultaneously.
description: Google Cloud Platform x4-1440-24t-metal is a bare-metal server in the x4 family, built on the x86_64 CPU architecture. It delivers 1440 dedicated vCPUs and 24576 GB of RAM, providing a high resource density of 17.07 GB of memory per core. The instance does not include local storage or integrated GPUs, relying entirely on external network storage for data persistence. This server is designed for massive enterprise workloads, offering a consolidated hardware profile that eliminates virtualization overhead. It is suited for large-scale in-memory databases, enterprise resource planning systems, and intensive data-processing tasks that require substantial memory capacity and parallel processing power.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform x4-1440-24t-metal is a bare-metal server featuring 1440 dedicated vCPUs, 24576 GB of RAM, and an x86_64 architecture, designed for massive in-memory enterprise workloads.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform x4-1440-24t-metal is a bare-metal server with 1440 dedicated vCPUs and 24576 GB of RAM for large-scale in-memory workloads.
tagline: A massive bare-metal instance featuring over twenty-four terabytes of memory and fourteen hundred dedicated cores for enterprise in-memory workloads.
bullet_points: 24576 GB of system memory providing 17.07 GB per core
No local storage or integrated GPUs included
Suited for large-scale in-memory databases and enterprise ERP systems
